signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Middle East escalation drives safe-haven demand — JPY COT LF net short −65,309 (crowded carry). USD/JPY at 159.72; 160.00 is the key level that has previously triggered BoJ verbal warnings; intervention risk rises on a sustained break above 160. Risk-off flow historically unwinds JPY shorts — 157.00 is the first technical level on a sustained risk-off move.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + Retail
signals.text: AUD/USD COT LF net long +41,675 vs retail 89% short — institutional vs retail divergence at extreme. HV30 at 8.2%, ATM IV (FXE chain) at 9.4% — options pricing a 1.2 vol-pt premium over realized. 25d RR at −0.14: mild put skew despite the bullish price trend — market hedging rather than chasing. Late-trend caution signal. A LF delta turning positive would invalidate the caution read.

signals.title: GBP/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: BoE 4.50% vs Fed 3.75% — 75bp GBP carry premium supports the pair structurally. GBP LF net long +28,426, AM constructive — positioning aligned with the carry read. GBP/USD at 1.3495 mid-range; 1.3600 is the next resistance on a continuation.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Positioning + Vol
signals.text: EUR LF net short −72 vs HV30 at 8.2%, ATM IV (FXE chain) at 9.4% — options pricing a 1.2 vol-pt premium over realized. 25d RR at −0.05: neutral skew. EUR/USD at 1.1685; positioning stalling — late-trend caution signal.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT + OIS
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short −17,090 (specs short) vs RBNZ OIS hike 100% priced. Specs are fading a hawkish CB. If RBNZ delivers or signals hike, short squeeze risk is elevated.
